macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed's Mary Daly Downplays Policy Division, Emphasizes FOMC Consensus on Steady Rates
items.summary: San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly stated that internal divisions are less important than the FOMC's unified decision to hold interest rates steady, noting it's too early to determine if the rate-cutting cycle has ended while monitoring inflation expectations.

items.title: Data Shows Tariffs Caused Economic Damage: Higher Inflation, Weaker Hiring, Manufacturing Decline
items.summary: One year post-'Liberation Day,' tariffs have led to accelerated inflation at 3% YoY, stalled job growth with manufacturing employment falling, higher household costs, and market volatility, as confirmed by chief economist Mark Zandi.

items.title: Gas Prices Surge to $4.54 National Average Amid US-Iran Tensions, Hitting Low-Income Households Hardest
items.summary: AAA reports national average gas at $4.536 due to Iran conflict, with LA nearing $9/gallon; Chevron CEO warns of global oil shortages from Strait of Hormuz issues, up 41% in prices, squeezing consumer budgets.

items.title: US National Debt Surpasses 100% of GDP for First Time Since WWII
items.summary: Publicly held US debt exceeded economy size in March, reaching over 100% of GDP amid ongoing fiscal pressures.

items.title: Q1 GDP Grows 2%, Slightly Below Expectations; AI and Consumer Spending Drive Gains
items.summary: First quarter GDP expanded at 2% versus 2.3% forecast, supported by equipment investment in AI/computers and consumer spending.

items.title: ADP Employment Adds 109K Jobs, Beating Estimates; Prior Month Revised Down
items.summary: US private payrolls rose 109K in latest report, above 99K expected, though previous month revised to 62K from higher prior figure.

items.title: 30-Year Mortgage Rate Rises to 6.3%
items.summary: Freddie Mac data shows average 30-year fixed mortgage rate up to 6.3% from 6.23% last week.

items.title: PCE Inflation Rises and Remains Elevated in March Due to Iran War Impact
items.summary: Commerce Department reports latest PCE data shows inflation increasing, partly from rising fuel costs tied to Iran conflict.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: $253 Million in Crypto Longs Liquidated in 24 Hours Amid Volatility
items.summary: Over $253 million in long positions were liquidated across major cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin and altcoins in the past 24 hours, intensifying market volatility and highlighting risks of leveraged trading.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $80K Amid Iran-U.S. Uncertainty Despite $1B ETF Inflows
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped 1.5-1.63% to around $79,552 in the last 24 hours due to US-Iran tensions, with Ethereum down 2% to $2,279; major altcoins like BNB, XRP, and Dogecoin also declined.

items.title: Crypto Futures Market Sees $250 Million in Liquidations, BTC and ETH Lead
items.summary: The crypto futures market experienced $250 million in liquidations over 24 hours, primarily in BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP, underscoring ongoing market volatility.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $80,000 as US Military Strikes Iran
items.summary: Bitcoin fell below $80,000 following US strikes on Iranian military facilities in retaliation for Navy attacks, contributing to broader crypto market declines including Ethereum and altcoins.

items.title: Germany Considers Ending 1-Year Tax-Free Crypto Holding Period
items.summary: Germany is eyeing the removal of its 1-year tax-free crypto holding period, aiming to generate $2.3 billion in revenue by 2027.

items.title: Kazakhstan Central Bank Plans $350M Bitcoin and Crypto Purchase
items.summary: Kazakhstan's central bank announced plans to purchase $350 million in Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies, signaling growing institutional interest in digital assets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Launches 'Dreaming' Feature for Claude AI
items.summary: Anthropic introduced 'Dreaming' in Claude AI, enabling it to revisit past conversations, identify patterns, clean memories, learn from mistakes, and improve automatically over time. This self-learning capability advances AI towards long-term memory and reflection.

items.title: Anthropic Announces 'Outcomes' and Doubled Rate Limits at Code with Claude Conference
items.summary: At the 'Code with Claude' developer conference, Anthropic revealed 'Outcomes' for developers to define success rubrics with Claude self-evaluating and retrying work, alongside doubled rate limits, infinite context windows, and multi-agent coordination.
items.source: YouTube - Claude's New 'Infinite' Context Window Model

items.title: Anthropic Research Warns AI Could Self-Improve by 2028
items.summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark warns in an interview that by 2028, AI systems may be capable of improving and building better versions of themselves, highlighting rapid advancements in AI capabilities.
items.source: YouTube - Axios Interview with Jack Clark

items.title: SubQ Model Claims to Surpass Claude, GPT, and Gemini with 12M Token Context
items.summary: SubQ introduces a sub-quadratic sparse-attention architecture for long-context tasks, supporting 12 million token reasoning at 150 tokens per second, outperforming top models like Claude Opus and GPT-5 variants on benchmarks.
items.source: YouTube - The AI Model Replace Claude, GPT & Gemini

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Tarkov TV Returns Tomorrow with Icebreaker Event Updates
items.summary: Escape from Tarkov developers announce Tarkov TV episode tomorrow, covering the ongoing Icebreaker event, potential release details, Scav Life DLC, new quests, and community questions.

items.title: FlatOut 4 Total Insanity VR Enters Early Access on Steam
items.summary: The VR version of the arcade racing game FlatOut 4 Total Insanity launches in early access, promising explosive crashes, intense races, and immersive first-person stunts.

items.title: Multiple New Indie Games Released on PS4 Store
items.summary: Recent PS4 store releases include Golem Lights (yesterday) and Axe Cop (two days ago), amid concerns about Sony potentially impacting the aging platform's digital marketplace.

items.title: Solo Flawless Grandmaster Clear in Destiny 2 Renegades GM Alert
items.summary: A player achieves solo flawless clear of Grandmaster Birthplace of the Vile using Arc Warlock, highlighting feedback on limited rotation and daily modifiers in Destiny 2's endgame activity.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Jeff Clarke Takes Over Dell's PC Business to Reverse Decline
items.summary: Jeff Clarke is leading Dell's PC division to reverse years of decline, restore the XPS brand prestige, and compete effectively in the evolving PC market.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple by scaling the mobile OS to desktops and reshaping personal computing.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Nano Nuclear Energy Signs MOU with Supermicro for AI Data Centers
items.summary: Nano Nuclear Energy has signed a memorandum of understanding with Supermicro Computer to provide nuclear power for data centers.

items.title: Hut 8 Secures $9.8 Billion Lease for Texas Data Center
items.summary: Hut 8 Corporation, transitioning from crypto mining, signed a 15-year lease worth at least $9.8 billion to provide computing power for a major tenant in a new Texas facility.
